title: Brockman acknowledges that Kimi K3 is quite good, but says OpenAI still holds a significant advantage
excerpt: A public conversation about the positions of Chinese and American large models was taken up for the first time by a core figure from OpenAI. On July 23, accordi
content_html:
A public conversation about the positions of Chinese and American large models was taken up for the first time by a core figure from OpenAI. On July 23, according to Bloomberg, Greg Brockman, president and co-founder of OpenAI, gave a rare and straightforward evaluation of Kimi K3, released by Moonshot AI in the previous week: this model is indeed very good, there's no doubt about it.

The weight of this statement lies in the fact that it comes from a key figure at a company long seen as a benchmark for AI in the United States. In the past, people generally assumed that the US had a significant lead over China in foundational models. However, the release of Kimi K3 is challenging this perception—Moonshot AI claims that its open-weight model has surpassed all competitors except Anthropic Claude Fable5 and OpenAI GPT-5.6. The market reaction followed quickly.

However, while acknowledging the strengths, Brockman left an open question. He said it was too early to determine whether Moonshot AI obtained the outputs of OpenAI models through "distillation" to train Kimi K3, and emphasized that OpenAI has always been monitoring such activities. This statement once again brought the technical boundary debate between open-weight models and native cutting-edge models back into the spotlight.

More intriguing was his quantitative judgment of the gap. Brockman cited some estimates stating that China may only be behind the US by about four months in AI model development, with other experts even suggesting the gap is smaller. As low-cost Chinese models spread rapidly and the capability gap continues to narrow, closed-source companies like OpenAI and Anthropic, which rely on subscriptions and API revenue, face more uncertainties—companies that are currently preparing for their IPOs and working to increase customer revenue.

Facing the approaching competitors, Brockman's confidence comes from two points: the substantial computing resources invested early on and years of accumulated AI research experience. Based on this, he believes that OpenAI still maintains a significant advantage over its competitors and stated that the company has been deeply studying how to build more efficient models that can precisely accomplish set goals.

He also took the opportunity to clarify a common misconception: open-weight models like Kimi are not free products. He reminded that these models are large in scale and require a lot of expensive computing power to run. The real competition is not about open source or not, but about who can build the most efficient, smartest, and best value-for-money models in specific tasks. When competitors approach the capability level with lower prices, what OpenAI needs to protect might no longer just be its position on model rankings, but an increasingly hard-to-define value advantage.

sentences.en: As low-cost Chinese models spread rapidly and the capability gap continues to narrow, closed-source companies like OpenAI and Anthropic, which rely on subscriptions and API revenue, face more uncertainties—companies that are currently preparing for their IPOs and working to increase customer revenue.
sentences.zh: 随着低成本中国模式的迅速普及和能力差距的不断缩小，像OpenAI和Anthropic这样依赖订阅和API收入的闭源公司面临更多的不确定性——目前正在准备IPO并努力增加客户收入的公司。

sentences.zh: 面对逼近的竞争对手，布罗克曼的信心来自两点：早期投入的大量计算资源和多年积累的人工智能研究经验。

sentences.en: Based on this, he believes that OpenAI still maintains a significant advantage over its competitors and stated that the company has been deeply studying how to build more efficient models that can precisely accomplish set goals.
sentences.zh: 基于此，他认为OpenAI相对于竞争对手仍然保持着显着的优势，并表示公司一直在深入研究如何构建更高效的模型，能够精确地完成既定目标。

The real competition is not about open source or not, but about who can build the most efficient, smartest, and best value-for-money models in specific tasks. When competitors approach the capability level with lower prices, what OpenAI needs to protect might no longer just be its position on model rankings, but an increasingly hard-to-define value advantage.

sentences.en: He also took the opportunity to clarify a common misconception: open-weight models like Kimi are not free products.
sentences.zh: 他还借此机会澄清了一个常见的误解：像 Kimi 这样的开放重量模型并不是免费产品。

sentences.en: He reminded that these models are large in scale and require a lot of expensive computing power to run.
sentences.zh: 他提醒说，这些模型规模很大，需要大量昂贵的计算能力才能运行。

sentences.en: The real competition is not about open source or not, but about who can build the most efficient, smartest, and best value-for-money models in specific tasks.
sentences.zh: 真正的竞争不在于开源与否，而在于谁能在特定任务中构建出最高效、最智能、性价比最高的模型。

sentences.en: When competitors approach the capability level with lower prices, what OpenAI needs to protect might no longer just be its position on model rankings, but an increasingly hard-to-define value advantage.
sentences.zh: 当竞争对手以更低的价格逼近能力水平时，OpenAI需要保护的可能不再只是其在模型排名上的地位，而是越来越难以定义的价值优势。

content_html_zh:
OpenAI核心人物首次就中美大模型立场进行公开对话。 7 月 23 日，据彭博社报道，OpenAI 总裁兼联合创始人 Greg Brockman 对 Moonshot AI 上周发布的 Kimi K3 给出了罕见而直白的评价：这个模型确实非常好，这是毫无疑问的。

这句话的分量在于，它来自一家长期被视为美国人工智能标杆的公司的关键人物。 过去，人们普遍认为美国在基础模型上明显领先于中国。 然而，Kimi K3 的发布正在挑战这种认知——Moonshot AI 声称其开放权重模型已经超越了除 Anthropic Claude Fable5 和 OpenAI GPT-5.6 之外的所有竞争对手。 市场反应很快。

然而，布罗克曼在承认其优势的同时，也留下了一个悬而未决的问题。 他表示，现在判断 Moonshot AI 是否通过“蒸馏”获得 OpenAI 模型的输出来训练 Kimi K3 还为时过早，并强调 OpenAI 一直在监控此类活动。 这一说法再次让开放权重模型与原生前沿模型之间的技术边界之争再次成为人们关注的焦点。

更有趣的是他对差距的定量判断。 布罗克曼引用了一些估计，称中国在人工智能模型开发方面可能只落后美国约四个月，其他专家甚至认为差距更小。 随着低成本中国模式的迅速普及和能力差距的不断缩小，像OpenAI和Anthropic这样依赖订阅和API收入的闭源公司面临更多的不确定性——目前正在准备IPO并努力增加客户收入的公司。

面对逼近的竞争对手，布罗克曼的信心来自两点：早期投入的大量计算资源和多年积累的人工智能研究经验。 基于此，他认为OpenAI相对于竞争对手仍然保持着显着的优势，并表示公司一直在深入研究如何构建更高效的模型，能够精确地完成既定目标。

他还借此机会澄清了一个常见的误解：像 Kimi 这样的开放重量模型并不是免费产品。 他提醒说，这些模型规模很大，需要大量昂贵的计算能力才能运行。 真正的竞争不在于开源与否，而在于谁能在特定任务中构建出最高效、最智能、性价比最高的模型。 当竞争对手以更低的价格逼近能力水平时，OpenAI需要保护的可能不再只是其在模型排名上的地位，而是越来越难以定义的价值优势。
